title: Well Productivity Index Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Well Productivity Index Inversion: Pressure transient analysis: infer permeability, skin factor, and reservoir boundaries from buildup/drawdown tests. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.darcy.radial_flow…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~25; wellbore_storage_distortion, variable_rate_history set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: p_ws(dt) = p* - (162.6*q*mu*B / (k*h)) * log10(dt + tp) - log10(tp) (Horner plot)
E.world_state_x: reservoir permeability k (mD), skin factor S, boundary distances
E.observation_y: wellbore pressure p_ws(dt) during buildup/drawdown for N_points data points

W.regime: Existence of the recovered reservoir_property_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 25); wellbore_storage_distortion dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Measurement gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: k estimate within 20% for clean buildup; skin within ±1 unit. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~25. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |wellbore_storage_distortion|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_points) for derivative calculation per iteration
